channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Four youths die in head-on motorcycle collision at Nedumangad
item.description: On the night of Sunday, March 9, 2026, a tragic road accident occurred at Nedumangad in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, leading to the death of four young men. The mishap took place around 10:45 ...

item.title: Government assures public: Supply is stable, avoid panic buying
item.description: The Presidential Palace has reassured the public that there is no need to rush into panic buying. Government officials emphasized that essential goods and commodities remain sufficiently available in ...

item.title: How Jesse Jackson's Legacy Inspires Hope and Action Against Corruption
item.description: The late Reverend Jesse Jackson’s legacy resonates far beyond the United States. When he ran for president in 1984, South Africans living under apartheid found hope in his courage and conviction, show...

item.title: Common Nigerian Foods That May Contribute to Erectile Dysfunction When Eaten in Excess and Healthier Alternatives
item.description: This article from Pulse.ng explains say erectile dysfunction (ED) no be small thing, as e fit show early sign of heart or blood vessel problems because erection need good blood flow. Many Nigerian men...

item.title: The Nightly Lives and Flight of Tawny Frogmouths in Melbourne
item.description: Tawny frogmouths, often mistaken for comical tree-stump lookalikes due to their wide, expressive eyes, have a fascinating nocturnal life that few get to see. Observing a breeding pair at Hopetoun Gard...

item.title: Legal profession urges government to reconsider plans to limit jury trials
item.description: Over 3,200 legal professionals, including 300 leading barristers and retired judges, have urged Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to abandon plans to restrict jury trials in England and Wales. The propo...

item.title: Samsung Explores AI-Based Customization for Galaxy Phones
item.description: Samsung is exploring a feature called 'vibe coding' for future Galaxy phones, which would allow users to modify apps or the user interface using natural language commands. The idea is to let AI genera...

item.title: Abuja Waste Contractors Urged to Improve Service Delivery
item.description: The Abuja Environmental Protection Board (AEPB) don yan say di performance of di waste evacuation and cleaning contractors wey dey operate under Federal Capital Territory Administration no dey reach e...

item.title: Police Seize 181kg of Suspected Cannabis on Webuye-Kitale Highway
item.description: Nairobi — Police in Kenya successfully intercepted a vehicle carrying 181 kilograms of suspected cannabis along the Webuye-Kitale Highway, in an operation aimed at disrupting drug trafficking networks...

item.title: South Africa Considers Interest Rate Hike Amid Rising Oil Prices and Middle East Conflict
item.description: The ongoing war involving the USA, Israel, and Iran in the Middle East is causing significant volatility in global oil prices, with Brent Crude jumping from $92.69 to around $107 per barrel. South Afr...

item.title: Tommy DeCarlo, Boston's lead singer, passes away at 60 after battling cancer
item.description: Tommy DeCarlo, the lead singer of the rock band Boston, has passed away at the age of 60 following a battle with brain cancer. Diagnosed in September, DeCarlo underwent emergency surgery after sufferi...

item.title: Malacañang assures sufficient supply of fuel and basic goods despite Middle East tensions
item.description: Malacañang has reassured the public that there is no need for panic buying in light of the ongoing Middle East crisis, emphasizing that the country has sufficient supplies of fuel and basic commoditie...

item.title: Global Oil Prices Volatile Amid Iran Leadership Change and Middle East Tensions
item.description: On Monday, global oil markets faced extreme volatility as Brent crude prices soared to $119.50 per barrel before retreating to around $82, following renewed Iranian strikes on Gulf energy facilities. ...

item.title: Oil resurfaces as a key factor in global geopolitics amid Middle East tensions
item.description: In 2026, oil has once again demonstrated its enduring influence on global politics and the economy. Despite a smaller share of global energy coming from oil compared to the 1970s, consumption has near...

item.title: Zanzibar records progress in trade and industrial development during Mwinyi’s first 100 days
item.description: Zanzibar Ministry of Trade and Industrial Development has shown major progress in the first 100 days of executing the 2025–2030 CCM Election Manifesto. Acting Minister Shariff Ali Shariff reported tha...

item.title: Federal Ministry of Education Assessment Team Visits Bonny Polytechnic for Development Review

item.title: Government Consults Opposition on Early Implementation of Women’s Reservation Act
item.description: The Government of India has begun informal consultations with Opposition leaders regarding the implementation of the Constitution (One Hundred and Sixth Amendment) Act, also known as the Women’s Reser...

item.title: Pakistan announces austerity measures including four-day work week to manage oil crisis
item.description: Due to the ongoing US-Israel conflict with Iran and the resulting surge in global oil prices, Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has introduced major austerity measures to protect the economy. K...

item.title: British public overwhelmingly opposes involvement in US-led war on Iran
item.description: Owen Jones argues that British political and media elites are repeating the mistakes of past foreign interventions, particularly those led by the United States, such as Iraq, Afghanistan, Libya, and G...

item.title: Raiders Revamp Roster with Linderbaum and Key Free Agent Signings
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders have launched an aggressive roster overhaul during the 2026 NFL legal tampering period, spending big to strengthen both offense and defense. The centerpiece of the spending is ce...
